summary: Hezbollah launched rockets toward the Western Galilee, most of which were intercepted with no casualties. Israel conducted multiple airstrikes against Hamas weapon depots in Gaza and Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon. The United States is reportedly considering a green‑light for Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets in Beirut while Israel appointed a new national security chief.
key_points: Hezbollah fired a barrage of rockets from Lebanon at the Western Galilee; most were intercepted by Israeli air defenses and the rest struck open areas with no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58333).
The IDF struck three Hamas weapon depots in the Gaza Strip, destroying explosives, rifles, sniper rifles and about ten Hamas pickup trucks (Source: @manniefabian/58339).
Israeli forces attacked Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including sites in the Bofar area and other southern Lebanese locations (Source: @almogboker78/17231; Source: @amitsegal/61544).

A car‑ramming attack at the Gush Etzion Junction injured four civilians, two seriously, and the attacker was neutralised by Israeli soldiers (Source: @manniefabian/58347; Source: @yediotnews25/130224).
U.S. officials hinted at granting Israel a “green light” to target Hezbollah positions in Beirut after previous restrictions (Source: @N12chat/210124).
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appointed Samuel Ben Ezra as head of the National Security Council, noting his background in missile defense development (Source: @inon_yttach/8453).
Red alerts were issued for the Lower Galilee following missile launch detections, with alerts activated in multiple localities (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67905).

sections.executive_summary: Hezbollah launched rockets toward the Western Galilee, most intercepted with no casualties. Israel intensified air operations, striking Hamas depots in Gaza and Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon. The United States is weighing a green‑light for Israeli strikes on Beirut while Israel reshuffled its national security leadership.

points: Iranian parliamentary security committee stated there is no commitment on the nuclear file toward the United States (Source: @naya_foriraq/76550).
Iranian state news reported President Masoud Pezeshkian sent a resignation letter to Supreme Leader Khamenei (Source: @shin_persian/6330).
Iranian officials indicated they will propose new amendments to the draft memorandum of understanding with the U.S. (Source: @asafroz/49700).
Iranian media reported a major security operation in Karbala with mass arrests of the Al‑Sarkhi group (Source: @naya_foriraq/76556).
Hezbollah fired rockets at the Western Galilee; most were intercepted, none caused injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58333).
IDF destroyed three Hamas weapon depots in Gaza, including ten pickup trucks used for arms transport (Source: @manniefabian/58339).
Israeli forces struck Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, targeting the Bofar ridge and other southern sites (Source: @almogboker78/17231; Source: @amitsegal/61544).
A car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction injured four civilians; the attacker was neutralised by IDF soldiers (Source: @manniefabian/58347; Source: @yediotnews25/130224).
Prime Minister Netanyahu appointed Samuel Ben Ezra as head of the National Security Council, noting his role in developing the Arrow‑3 missile system (Source: @inon_yttach/8453).
U.S. officials hinted at a green‑light for Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets in Beirut after previous delays (Source: @N12chat/210124).
IDF reported destruction of three Hamas weapon depots in Gaza, eliminating explosives, assault rifles, sniper rifles and ten pickup trucks (Source: @manniefabian/58339).
No civilian casualties were reported from the Gaza depot strikes according to the IDF statement (Source: @manniefabian/58339).
Hezbollah launched rockets from Lebanon toward the Western Galilee; most were intercepted (Source: @manniefabian/58333).
Israeli airstrikes targeted Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including the Bofar ridge and other southern locations (Source: @almogboker78/17231; Source: @amitsegal/61544).
A Lebanese official promised to disarm Hezbollah if Israeli attacks cease, while urging Israel to continue operations toward Beirut (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72226).
Red alerts were issued for Lower Galilee areas after missile launch detections, indicating possible cross‑border activity (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67905).
Iran reported jet activity over Karaj in Alborz Province (Source: @shin_persian/6335).
Explosions were reported in Tehran and Kermanshah, attributed to gas leaks by Iranian media (Source: @VahidOnline/75824).
A major security operation in Karbala, Iraq, led to mass arrests of the Al‑Sarkhi group (Source: @naya_foriraq/76556).
An explosion was geolocated in Shahriar, Iran, with damage to a building (Source: @shin_persian/6340).
IRGC Navy announced that 28 additional vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, passed through the Strait of Hormuz after receiving permission (Source: @presstv/192165).
No direct Gulf‑state military actions were reported in the provided messages.

key_developments: Hezbollah fired rockets at the Western Galilee; most were intercepted and no injuries occurred (Source: @manniefabian/58333).
The IDF destroyed three Hamas weapon depots in Gaza, eliminating explosives, rifles and ten pickup trucks (Source: @manniefabian/58339).
Israeli airstrikes hit Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including the Bofar ridge (Source: @almogboker78/17231).
A car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction injured four civilians; the attacker was neutralised (Source: @manniefabian/58347).
U.S. officials hinted at granting Israel a green‑light to strike Hezbollah targets in Beirut (Source: @N12chat/210124).
Samuel Ben Ezra was appointed head of Israel’s National Security Council, noted for leading Arrow‑3 missile development (Source: @inon_yttach/8453).
Red alerts were issued for Lower Galilee after missile launch detections, indicating heightened border tension (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67905).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to launch rockets toward northern Israel, testing Israeli air defenses while avoiding civilian casualties. Hamas retains significant weapon stockpiles in Gaza, prompting Israeli pre‑emptive strikes that could fuel further insurgent activity. Iran’s internal security operations and reported jet activity suggest a focus on regional power projection, while the U.S. consideration of a green‑light for Beirut strikes may raise the risk of broader escalation.
sections.regional_response: The United States appears poised to relax restrictions on Israeli operations against Hezbollah in Beirut, potentially altering the diplomatic balance. Iran’s statements on nuclear negotiations and internal security actions indicate a reluctance to engage in new concessions. Gulf states, through the IRGC’s vessel permissions, are maintaining maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a desire to keep strategic waterways open despite regional tensions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile launch detections in the Lower Galilee should be intensified to anticipate cross‑border attacks. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah’s rocket launch capabilities and Israeli retaliation patterns is essential. The appointment of a seasoned missile defense expert to Israel’s security council may signal upcoming advancements in aerial defense systems. Iranian internal security operations and jet activity warrant close observation for potential spillover effects into neighboring theaters.
